steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The team concludes previous assessments underestimated particle capacity. We need the choice that explains why prior studies came up short — specifically why they measured less than the true amount.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Most studies of microplastic accumulation on leaves have utilized extraction methods that do not have a substantial effect on epicuticular wax.": The experiment shows alkaline extraction (which removes wax) recovered 53.8%, while water recovered only 7.6%. If prior methods couldn't dissolve wax, they would have missed the particles trapped there — directly explaining the underestimation. ✓
  • "Most studies of microplastic accumulation on leaves have focused on forests with diverse tree species that exhibit varying levels of epicuticular wax.": Tree diversity is unrelated to the extraction-method gap the results reveal. ✗
  • "Most studies of microplastic accumulation on leaves have found particulate concentrations that greatly exceeded 53.8 percent.": This contradicts the data; 53.8% is the highest recovery shown. ✗
  • "Most studies of microplastic accumulation on leaves have involved the application of alkaline treatments before water and ultrasonic treatments rather than after them.": The order of treatments doesn't explain why wax-bound particles were missed. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
